lesson: A non-content FTS5 table has no delete triggers; deleting the source row leaves an orphaned FTS row unless you explicitly DELETE from the mirror too.
FTS5 MATCH throws on raw punctuation; sanitize queries to alphanumeric tokens joined with OR before passing them to MATCH.

Fuse lexical (FTS) and semantic (embedding) search with reciprocal rank fusion, then apply a cosine similarity floor on the fused candidates so irrelevant hits are dropped rather than merely re-ranked.

Calibrate a semantic relevance threshold empirically: measure cosine scores of known-irrelevant probes vs known-relevant pairs against the real corpus, then pick a cutoff that clears the noise ceiling.
Ground an LLM's self-reported success by actually executing its code in a subprocess with a timeout and checking the exit code, instead of trusting the model's own claim.

When distilling free-text lessons from an LLM, filter output with a vague-language blocklist plus a concrete-anchor allowlist (backticked code, dotted calls, CamelCase APIs, Big-O) so platitudes never pollute retrieval.
Dedup newly distilled lessons by embedding cosine similarity against all stored lessons before inserting, so near-duplicate phrasings don't bloat the memory store.
Make embedding calls soft-fail to None on network/timeout errors and have every downstream consumer degrade gracefully (lexical-only search, skip recall) rather than raising.
Open a fresh per-call SQLite connection with WAL journal mode and a busy_timeout rather than sharing one long-lived connection, to avoid lock contention across concurrent MCP tool calls.
Schema-migrate an existing SQLite DB by adding nullable columns idempotently (check PRAGMA table_info first) so old rows default to NULL and legacy code paths keep working unchanged.
